formulas.name: 1. Calculation of Force
formulas.variables: F = Force, m = mass, a = acceleration
formulas.units: Newton (N), kilogram (kg), meter per second squared (m/s²)
formulas.notes: To calculate the force applied on an object

formulas.name: 2. Calculation of Pressure
formulas.variables: P = Pressure, F = Force, A = Area
formulas.notes: To calculate the pressure exerted on a surface

formulas.name: 3. Calculation of Power
formulas.variables: P = Power, W = Work, t = Time
formulas.notes: To calculate the power output of an athlete

formulas.name: 4. Calculation of Energy
formulas.variables: E = Energy, W = Work, F = Force, d = Distance
formulas.notes: To calculate the energy expended by an athlete

formulas.name: 5. Calculation of Momentum
formulas.variables: p = Momentum, m = mass, v = velocity
formulas.units: kilogram meter per second (kg·m/s), kilogram (kg), meter per second (m/s)
formulas.notes: To calculate the momentum of an object

formulas.name: 6. Calculation of Torque
formulas.variables: τ = Torque, r = radius, F = Force
formulas.notes: To calculate the rotational force that causes an object to rotate

formulas.name: 7. Calculation of Angular Velocity
formulas.variables: ω = Angular Velocity, Δθ = Change in Angle, Δt = Change in Time
formulas.notes: To calculate the rate of change of angular displacement

formulas.name: 8. Calculation of Centripetal Force
formulas.variables: F_c = Centripetal Force, m = mass, v = velocity, r = radius
formulas.units: Newton (N), kilogram (kg), meter per second (m/s), meter (m)
formulas.notes: To calculate the force that acts towards the center of a circular path

formulas.name: 9. Calculation of Frictional Force
formulas.variables: F_f = Frictional Force, μ = Coefficient of Friction, N = Normal Force
formulas.notes: To calculate the force that opposes motion between two surfaces

formulas.name: 10. Calculation of Projectile Motion
formulas.variables: R = Range, v = initial velocity, θ = angle of projection, g = acceleration due to gravity
formulas.units: meter (m), meter per second (m/s), degree (°), meter per second squared (m/s²)
formulas.notes: To calculate the range of a projectile
